evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts` is a cross-feature mega-contract mixing DB-shaped fields (`thumbnail_url`, `created_at`, `shot_generation_id`) with UI/app aliases (`thumbUrl`, `createdAt`, `shotImageEntryId`) and legacy compatibility fields in one top-level type.
`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` embeds `GenerationRow[]`, pulling this broad mixed contract into core shot domain ownership rather than keeping translation at boundaries.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` acts as a global facade (normalize/log/notify + notifier configuration exports), and consumers like `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts`, and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` couple unrelated domains to one shared error pipeline.

Install UX ownership is split across `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/scenarios.ts`, and `src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/visuals.tsx` with string-key routing between files, making feature behavior depend on distributed conventions rather than a single bounded module.

`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` exposes both `getDataFreshnessManager()` and a `dataFreshnessManager` facade object that forwards every method via lazy lambdas, creating two public surfaces for one dependency.
`src/shared/hooks/useCompletedTimestamp.ts` is a pure re-export wrapper, while `useProcessingTimestamp` and `useTaskTimestamp` in timestamp hooks add thin forwarding layers with minimal new behavior.

`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` has `useDeleteFromTable` as a generic factory but its table union is only `'generation_variants'`, so the abstraction adds ceremony without polymorphic pressure.

Boundary helpers in edge request flow expose four different result contracts for similar request-validation tasks (`Response | null`, `EdgeOperationResult<T>`, `{ body?, errorResponse? }`, and plain object fallback), which pushes adapter glue into each caller (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:49-163`, `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts:1-47`).

Orchestrator-reference contract is not normalized at integration seams: writer emits `orchestration_contract` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/orchestrationContract.ts:28-45`) while billing readers/filters must query five historical paths (`supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:36-95`).

Async lifecycle semantics differ across task pipeline utilities: `runBatchTaskPipeline` resolves after settled task results (`src/shared/lib/tasks/batchTaskPipeline.ts:14-26`), but `preloadGenerationTaskMappings` resolves before nested `prefetchQuery` work is guaranteed complete because those promises are not awaited (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:176-195`).

Join-clips duration lookup is implemented through two unrelated boundary adapters with different failure behavior and observability (`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts:91-99,127-168` vs `src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts:12-38`).

`ensureUniqueFrame` increments `offset` by caller-provided `minGap`; because `minGap` is unchecked, `0` or negative values can cause non-terminating or invalid search behavior (`timelinePositionCalculator.ts`).
`timelinePositionCalculator.ts` aliases `MAX_FRAME_GAP` into `MAX_TIMELINE_FRAME_SPACING` and then threads both concepts through helpers, adding indirection without new behavior.
`useUpdatingTimestamp.ts` includes layered wrappers/doc-heavy scaffolding (`abbreviateTaskRelativeTime` + hook wrapper) for small formatting logic, increasing local cognitive load for a simple flow.
`ProjectImageSettingsInput` composes canonical settings with `Partial<LegacyProjectImageSettings>`, keeping deprecated fields in the accepted boundary type (`legacyProjectImageSettings.ts`).

`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput` composes canonical params with `Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput>`, continuing dual-shape ingestion instead of a hard migration boundary (`tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts`).
`uiSelectors.ts` contains only migration-style commentary with no exported selectors, indicating unfinished scaffold debt rather than an implemented contract.

supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts uses `Promise<unknown>` return types (`createGenerationFromTask`, `handleRegeneration`) and `let result: unknown`, which weakens compile-time guarantees for generation routing outcomes.

Cross-cutting task/data parsing is repeatedly expressed as `Record<string, unknown>` + ad-hoc narrowing in supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts, supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts, and src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts, indicating missing shared typed contracts.

src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts defines important event payload fields (for example `realtime:auth-heal.priority`) as unconstrained `string`, reducing type-level protection for infrastructure signaling.

src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts wraps `await supabase.rpc(...)` in try/catch but does not inspect returned `{ error }`, so RPC failures can bypass the handler and cleanup proceeds as if successful.

Frontend task creation modules (src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts) consistently `handleError(...); throw error;`, while other UI hooks (for example src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts, src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts) swallow after `handleError`, creating mixed propagation semantics.

An explicit edge failure result model exists in supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts, but complete-task flow still throws raw errors (supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts and supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts), producing inconsistent failure contracts at edge boundaries.

findings.summary: A single shared generation type mixes persistence, API, legacy, and UI concerns, weakening domain ownership boundaries.
evidence: `GenerationRow` contains both snake_case DB columns and camelCase app aliases plus legacy aliases in one interface.
`Shot.images?: GenerationRow[]` propagates this broad contract into shot domain surfaces.
Gallery handlers consume shot data via broad shape assumptions rather than a narrow, feature-owned view model.
findings.suggestion: Split `GenerationRow` into boundary-specific models (DB record, normalized domain entity, and UI view model), and constrain `Shot`/gallery layers to normalized domain or view-model types only.

findings.summary: Error handling is centralized as a catch-all shared facade, creating broad coupling across unrelated subsystems.
evidence: `handleError.ts` combines normalization, logging, toast dispatch, callback hooks, and notifier configuration exports in one shared entrypoint.
Mobile device detection code routes non-fatal environment errors into the global handler (`useMobile.*` contexts).
Gallery preloading and gallery action handlers also route local operational failures into the same global pipeline.
findings.suggestion: Introduce thin domain-level error adapters (e.g., `mobileErrorReporter`, `galleryErrorReporter`) over a lower-level core, and keep notifier wiring/configuration in a separate bootstrap module rather than the main call surface.

findings.summary: Install-instructions behavior is spread across modal, scenario resolution, and visual registry files with string-key contracts, reducing cohesion.
evidence: Modal decides runtime behavior via `resolveInstallVisualKey(...)` and then indexes `installVisualRegistry[visualKey]` from another file.
Scenario branching by platform/browser/device lives separately from visual ownership, coupled only by string literal union keys.
Title resolution and visual resolution are split functions with overlapping decision inputs, increasing change surface for one feature.
findings.suggestion: Create a single feature module that returns a complete install presentation model (title, instructions strategy, and component) from one boundary API, with internalized mapping instead of externally coordinated string keys.

findings.summary: The Supabase client module mixes singleton creation, runtime initialization, instrumentation installation, and debug-global exposure.
evidence: `client.ts` owns lazy singleton lifecycle and calls both bootstrap/runtime init paths.
The same module also gates instrumentation install and dev-only window global registration.
This concentrates operational and diagnostic responsibilities in one integration entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Separate runtime bootstrap, instrumentation wiring, and debug exposure into distinct modules, leaving `getSupabaseClient()` as a narrowly scoped acquisition API.

findings.summary: Data freshness manager exposes duplicate APIs (class accessor plus forwarding facade), adding indirection without behavioral gain.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager.ts` defines `getDataFreshnessManager()` and also `dataFreshnessManager` with 10 method-forwarding lambdas to the same singleton.
`useTasks.ts` depends on the facade object directly, so consumers can diverge between two equivalent access patterns.
findings.suggestion: Keep one canonical export (prefer a singleton instance or accessor), deprecate the duplicate facade, and migrate callers to one import contract.

findings.summary: Thin timestamp wrappers and re-export aliases create extra hook layers with little interface value.
evidence: `useCompletedTimestamp.ts` is only `export { useCompletedTimestamp } ...` from another module.
`useProcessingTimestamp` and `useTaskTimestamp` mostly repackage `useLiveRelativeTimestamp`/`useUpdatingTimestamp` with narrow formatter overrides, increasing surface area for similar semantics.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate timestamp hooks around one configurable primitive plus explicit formatter utilities, and remove alias-only exports after call-site migration.

findings.summary: Task orchestration metadata is written/read through multiple key paths, forcing defensive seam logic across task completion flows.
evidence: Payload builders populate both `orchestration_contract` and additional orchestrator fields nested in `orchestrator_details` or top-level params.
`orchestrator.ts` needs fallback OR filters across four JSON paths to find sibling tasks by orchestrator id/run id.
`generation.ts` documents routing extraction constraints and exceptions for passthrough payload sections, indicating non-uniform producer/consumer contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical orchestration metadata schema (single path, versioned if needed), add a boundary normalizer for legacy tasks, then simplify completion queries/extractors to that canonical path only.

findings.summary: Realtime instrumentation mixes unrelated runtime concerns in one function, making local behavior hard to reason about and test.
evidence: `installRealtimeInstrumentationImpl` performs object property interception, corruption event logging, and global console warning interception with dynamic import reconnect logic in one routine.
This local complexity mirrors another runtime-control module (`DataFreshnessManager`) and increases hidden coupling between connection health handling paths.
findings.suggestion: Split instrumentation into isolated installers (`installSocketReferenceTracking`, `installRealtimeDownInterceptor`) and compose them from a thin coordinator with explicit enable flags.

findings.summary: Edge request helpers use incompatible response contracts for equivalent boundary checks, creating avoidable glue code at call sites.
evidence: `ensureRequestMethod` returns `Promise<Response | null>` while parse helpers return either `EdgeOperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`, `{ body?: ..., errorResponse?: Response }`, or raw object fallback (`edgeRequest.ts:49-83,95-163`).
Shared result type already exists (`EdgeOperationResult`) but is only used for some of these entry-point checks (`edgeOperation.ts:19-47`).
findings.suggestion: Standardize request-boundary helpers on one contract (prefer `EdgeOperationResult<T>`), then provide a single adapter for HTTP response conversion so handlers can compose method-check + body-parse uniformly.

findings.summary: Task orchestration identity is written in one shape but read through multiple legacy shapes, indicating an unresolved cross-layer contract migration.
evidence: Contract builder emits `orchestration_contract.orchestrator_task_id` as canonical field (`orchestrationContract.ts:31-43`).
Billing extraction and sub-task lookup still depend on five alternative paths including legacy `originalParams` and top-level keys (`billing.ts:45-51,87-95`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single normalized orchestrator-ref accessor at ingestion time (or migration write-back), then collapse billing filters/readers to one canonical storage path and remove multi-path `.or()` queries after backfill.

findings.summary: `preloadGenerationTaskMappings` resolves before all downstream prefetches complete, so caller-visible completion semantics differ from other pipeline utilities.
evidence: `runBatchTaskPipeline` waits for all operations and settled results before returning (`batchTaskPipeline.ts:19-26`).

`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` kicks off `queryClient.prefetchQuery` inside `forEach` without awaiting, so function completion does not imply preloaded task data is ready (`generationTaskBridge.ts:176-195`).
findings.suggestion: Decide and document one completion contract: either await collected prefetch promises before resolving, or split API into `schedule...` and `await...` variants so consumers can explicitly choose fire-and-forget vs completion-guaranteed behavior.

findings.summary: Join-clips duration loading uses two unrelated adapters with different error propagation rules, producing inconsistent integration behavior across clip sources.
evidence: Pending-clip flow reads duration via raw `<video>` metadata and silently falls back to `0` with no structured error channel (`clipInitService.ts:91-99,145-168`).
Metadata manager flow uses `extractVideoMetadataFromUrl`, wraps in `OperationResult`, and emits centralized `handleError` on failure (`metadata.ts:12-38`).
findings.suggestion: Unify on one duration service (prefer `OperationResult`-based adapter) used by both pending-clip consumption and metadata refresh paths, so failures and fallback policy are consistent.

findings.summary: Canonical task/settings inputs still accept legacy fields via broad `Partial<Legacy...>` intersections, prolonging migration debt.
evidence: `ProjectImageSettingsInput = ProjectImageSettings & Partial<LegacyProjectImageSettings>` keeps deprecated fields valid at the type boundary.
`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput = TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput>` repeats the same migration pattern in another task pipeline.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy acceptance to explicit decoder/adaptor functions at ingestion boundaries, keep canonical exported input types legacy-free, and enforce sunset by rejecting legacy-only fields after a date-guarded feature flag.

findings.summary: `uiSelectors.ts` is a generated-looking contract stub with no implementation, creating misleading architecture signals.
evidence: `uiSelectors.ts` only contains comments about centralized selectors and exports nothing.
Adjacent centralization modules like `storageKeys.ts` provide real exported constants, highlighting the stub as incomplete migration residue.
findings.suggestion: Either implement and migrate at least one real selector constant set into `uiSelectors.ts` immediately or delete the file until adoption starts, to avoid false affordances.

findings.summary: Frame collision search uses unchecked step size and unnecessary constant indirection, making a core utility more fragile than needed.
evidence: `ensureUniqueFrame` updates `offset += minGap` without validating `minGap > 0`; invalid input can stall the loop despite `maxOffset` guard.
`MAX_TIMELINE_FRAME_SPACING` is a local alias for imported `MAX_FRAME_GAP`, adding one more name for the same value with no semantic distinction.
findings.suggestion: Clamp `minGap` to `Math.max(1, Math.floor(minGap || 1))` at function entry and remove the alias by using `MAX_FRAME_GAP` directly to reduce indirection.

findings.summary: Shared hook files are feature re-export aliases, creating unclear ownership boundaries between `shared` and `features`.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useBottomOffset.ts` only exports from `@/features/layout/hooks/useBottomOffset`.
`src/shared/hooks/useCompletedTimestamp.ts` only exports from `@/features/tasks/hooks/useCompletedTimestamp`.
The shared test imports `../useBottomOffset`, so package-level test placement validates a shared alias instead of the owning feature module.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical boundary: either move these hooks fully into `shared` (implementation + tests) or remove shared shims and import from feature-owned paths directly. Update imports/tests to the canonical location and document the rule for future hook placement.

findings.summary: Shared component tests currently assert export presence only, which leaves shared-layer responsibility boundaries weakly specified.
evidence: `AuthGate.test.tsx` only checks `expect(AuthGate).toBeDefined()`.
`BatchDropZone.test.tsx` only checks `expect(BatchDropZone).toBeDefined()`.
These tests do not validate behavioral contracts that would justify why these modules belong in `shared` versus feature-specific locations.
findings.suggestion: Replace existence-only tests with contract-focused tests (for example, auth-loading gating behavior for `AuthGate`) and use those contracts to enforce whether each component should remain in `shared` or be moved to feature-scoped directories.

findings.summary: GenerationRow is a multi-era compatibility bucket rather than a bounded domain contract
evidence: GenerationRow includes both current and legacy aliases for the same concepts (id vs shotImageEntryId vs shot_generation_id, thumbUrl vs thumbnail_url, createdAt vs created_at).
Type comment says it is the base for galleries/lightboxes/timeline, so one shared type absorbs multiple subsystem contracts instead of boundary-specific models.
findings.suggestion: Split GenerationRow into a canonical core type plus explicit boundary adapters (e.g., DB row shape, UI view model, legacy compatibility mapper). Keep legacy aliases confined to adapter layers and mark the canonical type as the only writable contract.

findings.summary: README governance rules rely on convention without enforceable module boundaries
evidence: README defines runtime contracts such as canonical Supabase access and bootstrap-scoped error notification assumptions.

Reviewed modules expose broad entrypoints (getSupabaseClient + initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals; handleError + configureErrorNotifier re-export) but contain no code-level policy guardrails preventing misuse from arbitrary call sites.
findings.suggestion: Convert README runtime contracts into enforceable architecture constraints: narrow public exports to policy-safe entrypoints, move privileged/debug configuration behind explicitly internal modules, and add static checks (lint/dep rules) that block disallowed import paths.

findings.summary: Central error module mixes multiple architectural responsibilities in one public surface
evidence: handleError.ts exports notifier configuration types/functions and also operational handlers (handleError, handleAndRethrow), while also doing normalization, logging, and notification dispatch in one flow.
README positions notifier wiring as bootstrap concern, but the same module is the ubiquitous runtime error entrypoint, which blurs ownership boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Separate configuration API from runtime handling API into distinct modules (e.g., errorHandling/config and errorHandling/runtime) and keep shared call sites importing only runtime helpers to preserve clear ownership boundaries.

findings.summary: Referral finalization misses Supabase RPC error path by relying on exceptions instead of checking returned `error`.
evidence: useAuthReferralFinalize calls `await supabase.rpc('create_referral_from_session', ...)` inside try/catch but never destructures/checks `error`; Supabase RPC failures are commonly returned in the response object.
By contrast, useGenerationMutations consistently checks `{ data, error }` and throws on `error`, ensuring failures are propagated.
findings.suggestion: Capture the RPC response (`const { error } = await supabase.rpc(...)`) and explicitly route non-null `error` through `handleError` (and optionally rethrow or return a failure result) before cleanup decisions.

findings.summary: UI async handlers mix fail-fast and swallow-after-log patterns, making caller behavior unpredictable.
evidence: Task creators consistently `handleError(...); throw error;` to preserve rejection semantics.
Other hooks (for example useDiscordSignIn and useMediaGalleryItemState) catch, call `handleError`, and then resolve without rethrowing or returning an explicit failure value.
This creates inconsistent expectations for consumers about whether failures are observable via rejected promises.
findings.suggestion: Standardize async UI contracts per layer: either always rethrow after `handleError`, or return an explicit `Result` shape (`{ ok: boolean, error?: ... }`) and apply it consistently across hooks.

findings.summary: Edge handlers define a structured failure contract but complete-task modules still throw raw errors.
evidence: edgeOperation.ts provides `EdgeOperationResult` with policy/recoverability/errorCode.
orchestrator.ts throws multiple `new Error(...)` instances for operational failures.
generation.ts catches and logs, then rethrows raw errors (`throw error`).
These paths bypass the structured policy metadata intended for consistent edge failure handling.
findings.suggestion: Adopt `EdgeOperationResult` (or a single equivalent envelope) in complete-task modules and convert throw-based failures into structured results at boundary points, preserving error policy metadata consistently.

findings.summary: Generation completion routing uses `unknown` for core return values, weakening compile-time safety for downstream consumers.
evidence: generation.ts declares `createGenerationFromTask(...): Promise<unknown>`, `handleRegeneration(...): Promise<unknown>`, and uses `let result: unknown` for branch outputs.
Sibling edge modules (orchestrator.ts, calculate-task-cost/index.ts) already define concrete interfaces for DB rows and task context, showing typed contracts are feasible in this subsystem.
Using `unknown` at this routing boundary forces runtime assumptions and prevents discriminated handling of generation outcomes at compile time.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared discriminated union for generation routing outcomes (for example `{ kind: 'none' } | { kind: 'generation', id: string } | { kind: 'variant', id: string }`) and replace `unknown` return variables/signatures accordingly.
